title: Reader Outcomes, Publisher Stewardship, and Service Charter · Execution Trace: Feed Go Reader Decision
summary: A coalition asks for a blog aggregator but has not named its readers, publisher obligations, freshness promise, moderation boundary, accessibility needs, or evidence of usefulness. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go reader decision increment in reader-centered feed-service charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Define reader group, decision, task journey, freshness need, harm, and observable value before choosing architecture.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Reader Decision · Theory Feed Go Reader Decision
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6717 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a useful reader and publisher service charter on observable decisions and inclusive evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Build Feed Go Reader Decision evidence · Theory Feed Go Reader Decision
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-reader-decision-qhjrmx" that uses case 8237 to gate a useful reader and publisher service charter on observable decisions and inclusive evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
